2026 TV Calendar: Archive of Past Dates

Did you miss anything? Here are the shows that premiered over the past month.
by Jason Dietz — September 8, 2023￨Updated June 12, 2026 at 4:28 PM PDT

Below, find shows that already debuted in recent weeks. For new and upcoming TV release dates, visit our current TV Premiere Calendar.

($) = VOD and/or digital release (requires rental/purchase)

THU / June 11

tbdThe Evil Lawyer Trailer
Foreign/Drama		Netflix

Funny Thing About Love
Drama		ALLBLK

Mark vs. The Mountain
Reality		GAPF
tbdSurviving Earth Trailer
Documentary/Nature: This eight-part nature series examines how various forms of life navigated survival during a variety of environmental crises dating back to 450 million years ago.		NBC 8p
Peacock (6/12)

Sweet Magnolias - Trailer
Drama		Netflix

Viral Hit Trailer
Foreign/Drama		Netflix

All the Queen's Men
Drama: The Tyler Perry series moves from BET+ to Paramount+ for its fifth and final season.		Paramount+

Reality		ID 8p
HBO Max
tbdColors of Evil: Black
Foreign/Drama/Thriller		Netflix

Drama: All eight episodes of Amazon's series adaptation of Carley Fortune's best-selling romance novel Every Summer After stream today.		Prime Video

MON / June 8

66Alice and Steve Trailer
Comedy: In this British "anti-romantic comedy" series created by Sex Education writer Sophie Goodhart, best friends Alice (Nicola Walker) and Steve (Jemaine Clement) begin feuding when the latter begins dating Alice's 26-year-old daughter Izzy (Yali Topol Margalith). The series just won the top award at Canneseries (the annual international TV festival held in Cannes just prior to the better-known film fest). All six episodes stream today.		Hulu

American Ninja Warrior
Reality Competition		NBC 9p
Peacock (6/9)

Reality		Bravo 8p

Documentary/Music: Questlove's latest music documentary focuses on the Grammy-winning band behind such 1970s hits as "Shining Star," "September," and "Boogie Wonderland."		HBO 9p

Drama: The series formerly known as Interview With the Vampire returns for its third season. Following each episode will be a new aftershow, The Vampire Lestat: After Dark.		AMC 9p

tbdAmong Us Trailer
Animation/Comedy		Paramount+

Belle Collective
Reality		OWN 8p
tbd($) The Birthday Party Trailer
Drama		RENT/BUY
68Cape Fear Trailer
Drama: Inspired by the 1957 novel The Executioners as well as its two prior film adaptations (also as "Cape Fear") in 1962 and 1991, Apple's 10-episode thriller finds a happily married couple (Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson) stalked by a man from their past: a killer who has just been released from prison (Javier Bardem). Martin Scorsese and Steven Spielberg, who directed and produced the 1991 film, respectively, both serve as producers here, while Nick Antosca (The Act) serves as creator and showrunner. CCH Pounder, Patrick Fischler, Jamie Hector, and Ron Perlman also appear in the series, while directors include Morten Tyldum, Trey Edward Shults, SJ Clarkson, and Jon S. Baird. Two episodes stream today.		Apple TV
tbd($) Chum Trailer
Thriller		RENT/BUY

Everything on the Menu with Braun Strowman - Trailer
Reality		USA 11p

Finding Mango: To Live and Die on Skid Row - Trailer
Documentary Special		Disney+/Hulu
tbdGroundswell Trailer
Documentary		Prime Video

Hannah Berner: None of My Business - Trailer
Comedy Special		Hulu

Her Husband's Double Life
Drama		LMN 8p
tbdThe Marked Woman Trailer
Foreign/Drama/Thriller		Netflix
52Mexico 86 Trailer
Foreign/Comedy/Drama		Netflix
52Office Romance Trailer
Rom-Com: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein—you know them better as J.Lo and B.Go—headline this raunchy, straight-to-streaming rom-com directed by Ol Parker (Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again). Lopez plays the CEO of an airline who finds herself attracted to one of her new employees (Goldstein) and tempted to violate her own anti-fraternization policy. Betty Gilpin, Edward James Olmos, Tony Hale, Amy Sedaris, and Bradley Whitford also star. In addition to starring, Goldstein wrote the screenplay alongside his fellow Ted Lasso co-creator Joe Kelly.		Netflix

Love Island [USA]
Reality		Peacock
53Not Suitable for Work Trailer
Comedy: The latest comedy series from Mindy Kaling re-teams her with producer Charlie Grandy (who also worked on Kaling's The Sex Lives of College Girls and The Mindy Project) for a look at five work-obsessed 20-somethings living in New York's Murray Hill neighborhood. (It sounds a bit like the flip side to FX's Adults, whose 20-somethings are obsessed with everything but their careers.) Ella Hunt, Avantika, Will Angus, Jack Martin, Nicholas Duvernay, and Jay Ellis head the cast, while recurring guests include Victor Garber, Ego Nwodim, Constance Wu, and Greg Germann. Three episodes stream today, and you'll get two more each Tuesday through June 23.		Hulu

Password
Game Show		NBC 10p
Peacock (6/3)

Reality		ID 9p

Reality		WE 8p
62Miss You, Love You Trailer
Drama: Allison Janney stars as a grieving widow who forms an unlikely bond with her estranged son's assistant (Andrew Rannells) as the two make arrangements for her husband's funeral. Written and directed by Jim Rash (Community), the film also sars Bonnie Hunt, Suzy Nakamura, and Oscar Nuñez.		HBO 8p
HBO Max
tbd($) Nesting Trailer
Thriller		RENT/BUY
49Propeller One-Way Night Coach Trailer
Adventure/Drama/Family: John Travolta writes and directs a straight-to-streaming adaptation of his own 1997 children's book that follows a young boy and his mother on a magical cross-country flight to Hollywood at the dawn of commercial aviation.		Apple TV
71Rafa Trailer
Foreign/Reality/Sports		Netflix
tbdSmothered Trailer
Foreign/Thriller		Shudder
80Star City Trailer
Drama/Sci-Fi: A spinoff from For All Mankind, Apple's latest sci-fi series examines the same basic story but from the perspective of the Soviet Union, with this first season serving as a prequel that depicts the USSR's frantic efforts to land a man on the moon ahead of the Americans in an alternative-history 1960s. Rhys Ifans, Anna Maxwell Martin, Alice Englert, and Agnes O'Casey head the cast. The first two (of eight) episodes stream today.		Apple TV

This Is Poly Trailer
Reality		WE 9:30p

Criminal Minds: Evolution - Trailer
Drama		Paramount+

Drama		NBC 8p
72A Good Girl's Guide to Murder - Trailer
Drama: Streaming in its entirety today, the BBC-produced show's six-episode second season adapts Holly Jackson's novel Good Girl, Bad Blood. 		Netflix

Concert Speical		Netflix

Animation/Family		Netflix

Mystery at Blind Frog Ranch
Reality		Discovery 10p

The Rise and Fall of the Roman Empire
Reality		History 8p
72Spider-Noir Color trailer - B&W trailer
Drama: The first regular TV gig for Nicolas Cage—and the first live-action Spider-Man series since the 1970s—finds him voicing Ben Reilly/The Spider, a grizzled, down-on-his-luck private investigator in 1930s New York, where he's also the city's only superhero. The adaptation of the Spider-Man Noir comics is produced by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller (among others) and also stars Lamorne Morris, Li Jun Li, Jack Huston, and Brendan Gleeson. All eight S1 episodes stream today—in both color and black-and-white versions—while the season will also continue to air weekly on Mondays on MGM+.		Prime Video

HBO Max
38Ladies First Trailer
Comedy: This English-language remake of a 2018 French-language Netflix original (I Am Not an Easy Man) stars Sacha Baron Cohen as a male chauvinist who awakens in a parallel universe in which gender roles are reversed. Rosamund Pike also stars, joined by Emily Mortimer, Charles Dance, Richard E. Grant, and Fiona Shaw in supporting roles.		Netflix
67Mating Season Trailer
Animation/Comedy: A raunchy new adult animated comedy from the team behind Netflix's long-running hit Big Mouth, Mating Season focuses on love, sex, and relationships ... among forest fauna. Nick Kroll, Zach Woods, June Diane Raphael, and Sabrina Jalees head the voice cast, while guests include Sarah Silverman, Vaness Bayer, Jason Mantzoukas, Jack McBrayer, Lisa Gilroy, Jason Alexander, Lena Waithe, Aidy Bryant, Abbi Jacobson, David Duchovny, Drew Tarver, Lauren Lapkus, and Timothy Olyphant.		Netflix
tbd($) Sick Puppy Trailer
Horror/Thriller		RENT/BUY
tbdSystem
Foreign/Drama		Prime Video

The Yogurt Shop Murders - Trailer
Reality: A new 90-minute episode follows recent developments in the true crime story examined in last year's miniseries.		HBO 9p
HBO Max

THU / May 21

71The Boroughs Trailer
Drama/Sci-Fi/Horror: Supernatural thriller The Boroughs comes from Jeffrey Addiss and Will Matthews, who previously developed the excellent Dark Crystal sequel series for Netflix in 2019. Set in a seemingly peaceful retirement community, the show follows a misfit group of retirees who band together to battle a mysterious, malevolent force. If that sounds a bit like a Stranger Things for the grey hair set, well, you're right: It is produced by Stranger Things creators the Duffer brothers. (Addiss and Matthews also cite the film Cocoon as an influence.) The excellent ensemble is led by Alfred Molina, Geena Davis, Alfre Woodard, Clarke Peters, Bill Pullman, and Denis O'Hare and also includes Ed Begley Jr., Jena Malone, Karan Soni, Rafael Casal, Dee Wallace, and Jane Kaczmarek. All eight episodes stream today.		Netflix

James.
Foreign/Reality/Sports		Netflix
tbdKyle Larson vs. The Double Trailer
Documentary/Sports		Prime Video

SkyMed - Trailer
Drama		Paramount+

Carizzma
Foreign/Comedy		Netflix

Desi Bling Trailer
Foreign/Reality		Netflix
38Jack Ryan: Ghost War Trailer
Action/Drama/Thriller: John Krasinski returns as Tom Clancy's hero Jack Ryan in this feature film follow-up to the recently concluded Prime Video series. Also returning from the series are Wendell Pierce, Michael Kelly, and Betty Gabriel.		Prime Video
tbdKylie Trailer
Reality/Music		Netflix
72Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed Trailer
Comedy/Drama: Apple's 10-episode, darkly comedic thriller stars Tatiana Maslany as a newly divorced mom caught in "a dangerous rabbit hole of blackmail, murder and youth soccer" after she becomes certain she has witnessed a crime and recklessly begins her own investigation. Also starring Jake Johnson, Murray Bartlett, and Brandon Flynn, the series comes from David J. Rosen (a writer on Sugar and Hunters) and counts David Gordon Green among its directors. Two half-hour episodes stream today followed by weekly installments through July 15.		Apple TV

The Oval
Drama		BET 9p
Paramount+

Comedy 		RENT/BUY
63Dutton Ranch Trailer
Drama: The fourth new Taylor Sheridan-produced series to arrive in the first half of this year, Dutton Ranch is also yet another spinoff (the fourth!) from Yellowstone. It finds Kelly Reilly and Cole Hauser reprising their roles as Beth Dutton and Rip Wheeler, respectively, though now they are in South Texas, where they do battle with a ruthless rival rancher. Ed Harris, Annette Bening, Jai Courtney, Finn Little, Juan Pablo Raba, and J.R. Villarreal also star.		Paramount+

Action/Thriller		RENT/BUY

61Good Omens - Trailer
Comedy/Drama/Fantasy: Delayed and severely shortened (to just a single 90-minute episode) after its creator (Neil Gaiman) landed in hot water, the third season of the David Tennant- and Michael Sheen-led fantasy series streams today, nearly three years since the show's second season debuted.		Prime Video

The Great American Road Rally: Celebrity Edition
Reality		CW 9p
71Off Campus Trailer
Drama: Is there room enough on television for a second steamy hockey romance? Adapted from a book series by Ella Kennedy, Off Campus follows the members of an elite college hockey team ... and the women who love them. Season 1 streams in its entirety today, and a second season is on the way.		Prime Video

Perfect Match - Trailer
Reality		Netflix

Roosters - Trailer

Reality/Sports: This five-part docuseries (airing weekly) spends four years following the United States Men's National Soccer Team as the squad prepares for this year's World Cup.		HBO 9p

Reality Competition		Food 9p
HBO Max (5/12)

Everyone Is Doing Great
Comedy: The coming-of-age comedy about a pair of former teen TV stars (James Lafferty, Stephen Colletti), which saw its debut season debut on Hulu over five years ago, now comes to Netflix for its previously filmed but never aired second season. (Netflix will also stream the first season beginning today.)		Netflix

The Great American Baking Show - Trailer
Reality Competition		The Roku Channel

I Literally Just Told You
Game Show		BritBox
tbdThe Island [An t-Eilean]
Drama 		Acorn TV

Pop Culture Jeopardy! - Trailer
Game Show: The Colin Jost-hosted spinoff moves to Netflix from Prime Video for its second season. New episodes stream daily on weekdays.		Netflix
tbdRegular Show: The Lost Tapes
Animation/Comedy/Family: The surreal animated series from JG Quintel, which originally ran for eight seasons (through 2017), is back with a follow-up series that returns much of the same cast (including Quintel, William Salyers, Sam Marin, Mark Hamill, Minty Lewis, and Janie Haddad Tompkins). The new series will also stream on both HBO Max and Hulu later this year.		Cartoon Network tbd

Foreign/Drama: This live-action Japanese series is adapted from the long-running manga Chiruran: Shinsengumi Requiem.		HBO Max

67Amadeus Trailer
Drama: Making its American debut (after airing in the UK last year) today with the first of five episodes, period drama Amadeus finds Will Sharpe rocking the title role as composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, while Paul Bettany plays his rival, Antonio Salieri. 		Starz [app/VOD only]

Foreign/Comedy		Prime Video
55Remarkably Bright Creatures Trailer
Drama: Sally Field, Lewis Pullman, Dan Payne, Kathy Baker, Joan Chen, Sofia Black-D'Elia, and Colm Meaney star in an adaptation of Shelby Van Pelt's best-selling novel about a widow who forms a bond with a giant Pacific octopus (and some humans, too) while working at an aquarium. Olivia Newman (Where the Crawdads Sing) directs from a script that she co-wrote with John Whittington (Sonic the Hedgehog 2 & 3). 		Netflix

RuPaul's Drag Race All Stars - Trailer
Reality Competition		Paramount+

The Secrets of the Matriarch
Drama		LMN 8p

Thank You, Next - Trailer

tbdUnconditional Trailer
Foreign/Drama: The eight-episode Israeli thriller about a young woman who is arrested for drug smuggling while vacationing with her mother in Russia launches with its first two episodes today.		Apple TV

tbdThe Chestnut Man: Hide and Seek - Trailer
Foreign/Drama		Netflix

The Dating App Nightmare
Drama		LMN 8p

Jersey Shore Family Vacation - Trailer
Reality		MTV 8p
76Legends Trailer
Drama: Based on a true story, this six-part British crime drama follows a group of ordinary UK customs officials selected for a dangerous undercover mission to infiltrate several major drug smuggling gangs in the early 1990s. The series comes from Neil Forsyth (The Gold) and stars Tom Burke and Steve Coogan.		Netflix
59M.I.A. Trailer
Drama: The latest crime drama from Ozark co-creator Bill Dubuque follows a young woman's journey to become a drug "queenpin" in South Florida while seeking revenge for the slaughter of her family.		Peacock
tbdMy Dearest Assassin
Foreign/Drama		Netflix
71The Terror: Devil in Silver - Trailer
Horror: A streaming exclusive for now (with a cable debut on AMC expected this fall), this third installment in AMC's horror anthology series The Terror (which last aired in 2019) adapts Victor LaValle's novel The Devil in Silver. The six-episode adaptation comes from Chris Cantwell (Halt & Catch Fire) and LaValle and stars Dan Stevens as a moving man who is wrongly committed to a hellish psychiatric institution, where he must plot a path to escape. CCH Pounder, Judith Light, Stephen Root, Aasif Mandvi, Marin Ireland, and John Benjamin Hickey also star, while directors include Karyn Kusama.		AMC+/Shudder

We Are Jeni - Trailer
Reality Special		ID 9p
HBO Max

WED / May 6


Busted on Bodycam Trailer
Reality		Tubi
tbdCitadel - Trailer
Drama: Season 2 of the Russo brothers' ambitious (yet mediocre) globetrotting spy thriller finds Matt Berry, Jack Reynor, and Merle Dandridge joining returning cast members Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Stanley Tucci, Richard Madden, Lesley Manville, and Ashleigh Cummings.		Prime Video
tbdLife on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure - Trailer
Documentary Special		PBS 8p

Love Is Blind: Poland Trailer
Foreign/Reality		Netflix
80The Other Bennet Sister Trailer
Drama: The BBC's series adaptation of Janice Hadlow's 2020 novel that brings new depth to the life of Pride and Prejudice middle sister Mary Bennet as she leaves her family home and heads to both London and the Lake District—finding love along the way—stars Ella Bruccoleri, Ruth Jones, Richard E. Grant, and Indira Varma. The first three half-hour episodes stream today, with the remaining seven arriving one per week.		BritBox

Worst Ex Ever - Trailer
Reality		Netflix

79Gary
Comedy/Drama Special: Today brings the surprise release of a new, hourlong episode of The Bear (listed separately on Hulu under "Gary"), which follows Ebon Moss-Bachrach's Richie and Jon Bernthal's Mikey on a work trip to Gary, Indiana. (It's a flashback, obviously.) 		Hulu

83Lord of the Flies Trailer
Drama: Making its American debut today after previously airing in the UK (where it received extremely positive reviews), this four-episode adaptation of William Golding's classic 1954 novel about a group of English schoolboys who turn to savagery after being stranded on a desert island (and, really, who could blame them—they weren't even allowed to bring their 10 favorite albums with them) comes from Adolescence creator Jack Thorne. 		Netflix

Reality		Tubi
61Man on Fire Trailer
Drama: Yahya Abdul-Mateen II stars as elite mercenary John Creasy, who must battle PTSD and his own personal demons, in this action-driven series adapted from the books by A.J. Quinnell. (You may remember a 2004 film adaptation starring Denzel Washington; then again, you probably don't.) The new adaptation comes from Kyle Killen (Halo), while Billie Boullet, Bobby Cannavale, Alice Braga, Scoot McNairy, and Paul Ben-Victor fill out the cast. All seven episodes stream today.		Netflix

Mysteries of Ancient Medicine
Reality		Nat Geo 9p

tbdShould I Marry a Murderer? Trailer
Reality		Netflix
78Widow's Bay Trailer
Comedy/Drama/Horror: In this horror comedy from Parks and Recreation writer Kate Dippold, Matthew Rhys plays the unimpressive mayor of a tiny New England island town whose drive to turn the low-key island into a tourist destination runs headlong into long-held local superstitions that turn out to be truer than anyone expected. Stephen Root, Kate O'Flynn, and Dale Dickey also star, while Hiro Murai (Atlanta, The Bear) directs half of this season's 10 episodes and produces. Episodes stream weekly through June 17 following today's three-episode premiere.		Apple TV

67Half Man Trailer
Drama: Co-produced by the BBC, this six-episode series comes from Baby Reindeer creator Richard Gadd. The series chronicles four decades in the often contentious relationship between estranged brothers played as adults by Gadd and Jamie Bell (and by a different pair of actors during flashbacks to their teenage years). 		HBO 9p
HBO Max

LOL: Last One Laughing Italy
Reality Competition		Prime Video
66Running Point - Trailer
Comedy		Netflix
53Stranger Things: Tales From '85 Trailer
Animation/Drama/Horror/Sci-Fi/Fantasy: The first spinoff from the recently concluded Netflix hit Stranger Things is an animated story set between the events of S2 and S3 of that series as the children of Hawkins battle a new terror that emerges from the winter ice. While the characters will be familiar (Will, Eleven, Dustin, etc.), their voices will come from an all-new cast that also includes Odessa A'zion, Janeane Garofalo, Lou Diamond Phillips, and Robert Englund in new roles. Tales From '85 is developed and run by franchise newcomer Eric Robles (Nickelodeon's Fanboy & Chum Chum), though the Duffer brothers and Shawn Levy return as producers.		Netflix

Sugarcreek Amish Mysteries
Drama		UP 9p
tbdYiya Murano: Death at Tea Time Trailer

88This Is a Gardening Show Trailer
Reality: Is Zach Galifianakis the new Monty Don? Well, not exactly. The comedian, an admitted gardening novice, hosts this lighthearted series in which he aims to grow his gardening knowledge alongside the viewing audience. Six episodes stream today.		Netflix

4x20: Quick Hits Trailer
Documentary/Anthology: Four short cannabis-related documentaries stream today, for no particular reason that we can think of. You can probably watch any one of the shorts in the time it takes to teleport to a Waffle House.		Hulu

Hollywood Demons - Trailer
Reality		ID 9p
HBO Max
62Kevin Trailer
Animation/Comedy: An adult animated comedy series created by Aubrey Plaza and Joe Wengert, Kevin centers on a housecat of the same name (voiced by Jason Schwartzman), who decides that he is tired of living with humans and opts to head out for life on his own in the big city, eventually joining up with a group of fellow misfit animals at a pet rescue. Plaza, Aparna Nancherla, John Waters, Whoopi Goldberg, Amy Sedaris, and Gil Ozeri fill out the main voice cast, while guests include Nicole Byer, Maria Bamford, Carl Tart, Eugene Cordero, Quinta Brunson, Lennon Parham, Mike Mitchell, Stephen Malkmus, Tig Notaro, and Stephanie Allynne. All eight episodes stream today.		Prime Video

Kill Tony: Wrestlemania
Comedy Special		Netflix

Drama		CW 8p

Artemis II: To the Moon and Back - Trailer
Special		Discovery 8p
77Beef - Trailer
Comedy/Drama: Following a widely acclaimed and Emmy-winning first season three years ago, the darkly comedic Netflix anthology series from Lee Sung Jin returns with an all-new story set in an elite country club owned by a Korean billionaire, whose fight with his wife (witnessed by a younger couple at the club) serves as the season's inciting incident. Season 2's cast is led by Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac and also includes Youn Yuh-jung, Song Kang-ho, Charles Melton, Cailee Spaeny, and William Fichtner. All eight episodes (which run around 30 minutes each) stream today.		Netflix

Big Mood - Trailer
Comedy		Tubi

Dandelion Trailer
Anime		Netflix
